contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Chat UX: diagnose and fix mobile readability on dapp.truesight.me/chat.html
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp/pull/179
  
  Details:
  UX testing and mobile-readability fixes for the Governor Chat page (`dapp.truesight.me/chat.html`). The page was unusable on phones — the conversation panel was being squeezed to roughly half of the viewport by stacked header chrome, and CSS authoring order was silently shadowing every mobile override.
  
  ## Diagnosis (the testing portion)
  
  Loaded `chat.html` against the live dev server, walked through the mobile and desktop viewports in Playwright (iPhone 13 / 390×664 and 1280×800), and identified the actual root causes of the "small / hard to read" complaint:
  
  1. **CSS cascade bug** — the `@media (max-width: 600px)` block was authored *before* the desktop defaults, so same-specificity rules below silently shadowed every mobile `h1`/`#description`/`.message` override. The mobile media query was effectively dead code for those selectors. Confirmed via the first screenshot (h1 still rendering at 1.8rem despite the mobile override declaring 1rem).
  2. **Six stacked header rows on mobile** (nav dropdown, TDG balance badge, logo, h1, italic description, status) consumed ~50% of the viewport before any conversation content appeared.
  3. **Sender label at 0.75rem** was too small to scan on phones.
  4. **Fixed 120px input max-height** truncated multi-line drafts and forced internal scrolling for typical messages.
  
  ## Modifications (the implementation portion)
  
  - Moved the `@media (max-width: 600px)` block to the end of `<style>` so mobile rules actually win the cascade.
  - On phones (≤600px): hide `#logo` and `#description`, shrink `<h1>` to a slim 1rem banner, hide `#status` when empty. Reclaims ~150px of vertical space.
  - Bumped message body to 1rem / 1.55 line-height and sender label from 0.75rem → 0.8rem (uppercase + letter-spacing) for readability on every viewport.
  - Auto-grow `#chat-input` up to 30vh as the user types, reset on send.
  
  ## Verification
  
  Re-captured iPhone 13 and 1280×800 screenshots after each change. Mobile conversation-panel utilization at default input height: ~52% before → ~74% after. With a 5-line draft in the input: ~52% → ~56% (no longer dominated by chrome). Desktop layout unchanged aside from the deliberate sender-label restyle. User confirmed live: "Verfied. Works well."
  
  ## Pull requests
  
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp/pull/179 — merged
- Contributor(s): Garyjob <garyjob@gmail.com>
